name: Abbaye de Saint-Martin du Canigou and Goa Tower - trail loop from Vernet-les-Bains - Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
name_original: Abbaye de Saint-Martin du Canigou et Tour de Goa – boucle trail depuis Vernet-les-Bains – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes
region_intro: Jogging around Vernet-les-Bains offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Canigou mountain within the Catalan Pyrenees Regional Nature Park. The region features the Cady Valley with its meandering river, leading to the Gorges du Cady and scenic waterfalls. An extensive network of footpaths provides numerous upward and downward routes, catering to various fitness levels. The village itself, designated as France's first "Village Arboretum," offers a unique environment for running amidst diverse plant species. Best jogging routes around Vernet-les-Bains The most popular jogging route is Abbaye de Saint-Martin du Canigou and Goa Tower - trail loop from Vernet-les-Bains - Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Catalanes, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete. This challenging route combines historical sites with significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local runners is Col de Jou – Refuge de Mariailles loop from Vernet-les-Bains, a difficult 15.2 miles (24.4 km) path. This route traverses mountainous terrain, offering expansive views of the Pyrenees. Local runners also love the Goa Tower – Col de Jou loop from Vernet-les-Bains, a 30.5 miles (49.0 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 8 hours 28 minutes. Jogging around Vernet-les-Bains is defined by the dramatic Canigou massif, the scenic Cady Valley, and an extensive network of footpaths. The region offers options for both challenging trail running and more moderate jogging routes. The routes in Vernet-les-Bains are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Vernet-les-Bains's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Vernet-les-Bains?
A: There are over 50 running routes around Vernet-les-Bains, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for beginners in Vernet-les-Bains?
A: While many routes in Vernet-les-Bains are challenging, there are also moderate options available. The region's extensive footpath network includes paths along the Cady River and through the 'Village Arboretum' that can be suitable for less experienced runners or those seeking a gentler pace. You can find 11 moderate routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes near Vernet-les-Bains?
A: The terrain around Vernet-les-Bains is incredibly diverse, ranging from shady paths along the Cady River in the valley to mountainous trails with significant elevation changes at the foot of the Canigou massif. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including the dramatic Gorges du Cady and the unique environment of the 'Village Arboretum' within the village itself.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Vernet-les-Bains?
A: The running routes in Vernet-les-Bains are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the dramatic scenery, the challenging ascents, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with exploring historical sites and natural landmarks.

Q: Can I find routes with waterfalls or scenic views in Vernet-les-Bains?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its natural beauty. You can jog along the Cady River to the spectacular Gorges du Cady, which features picturesque waterfalls like the Cascade des Anglais and Saint-Vincent Waterfall. Many mountainous routes also offer breathtaking views of the Pyrenees and the Mediterranean.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running trails?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974178469/abbaye-de-saint-martin-du-canigou-and-goa-tower-trail-loop-from-vernet-les-bains-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-catalanes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abbaye de Saint-Martin du Canigou and Goa Tower trail loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past both the historic Abbaye de Saint-Martin du Canigou and the ancient Tour de Goa. The village also offers the 'Kipling Circuit', a self-guided trail through the Belle Époque sector with cultural insights.

Q: What unique natural features can I discover while running in Vernet-les-Bains?
A: Vernet-les-Bains is France's first 'Village Arboretum', boasting over 2,000 identified trees of more than 300 different species. This creates a unique and pleasant environment for jogging within the village. Beyond that, the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canigou Massif&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic Cady Valley with its gorges are prominent natural highlights.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Vernet-les-Bains are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14954885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Jou – Refuge de Mariailles loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38628494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goa Tower – Col de Jou loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied distances and challenges.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails for endurance training?
A: Vernet-les-Bains is an excellent destination for long-distance trail running. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38628494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goa Tower – Col de Jou loop&lt;/a&gt; stretch for 49.0 km (30.5 miles) with significant elevation gain, providing ample challenge for endurance athletes.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running trails in the area?
A: The Catalan Pyrenees Regional Nature Park generally welcomes dogs on trails, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. It's always advisable to check specific trail regulations or local signage, but many of the natural paths are suitable for running with your canine companion.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Vernet-les-Bains?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter running is possible, particularly on lower trails, but higher mountain routes may require appropriate gear for snow and ice.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the running routes?
A: Vernet-les-Bains itself has various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your run. While specific cafes directly on mountain trails are rare, you'll find options within the village and sometimes at refuges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cortalets Refuge&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5676644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mariailles Refuge&lt;/a&gt; on longer routes.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of Mount Canigou?
A: Many routes in and around Vernet-les-Bains offer spectacular views of the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/885205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canigou Massif&lt;/a&gt;, which dominates the skyline. The mountain itself provides a dramatic backdrop for your run, and higher trails will give you expansive panoramas of the peak and surrounding Pyrenees.
tips: With a magnificent view, the Pic de l'Alzina is the objective of a 2-hour round trip hike from Vernet-les-Bains.

The Saint-Martin du Canigou Abbey, founded in the year 1001 by a Count of Cerdagne, is located in the heart of the Canigou massif, on a 1,094 meter high rocky outcrop over the Cady valley.

This oasis of peace is nestled in beautiful, peaceful and green surroundings, and can only be reached on foot from the village of Casteil, after a climb of approximately 40 minutes.

The Col de Jou peaks at an altitude of 1,125 meters. It is a well-known rallying point for local hikers. Indeed, the GR 10 passes through there, and it is the starting point for many hikes including the one that connects Canigou peak.

The Goa Tower is a 13th century medieval watchtower located 1264 m above sea level.
It made it possible to communicate by signals with a whole network of towers visible from each other. Its once strategic position now makes it an ideal position to admire the landscape.

Pic de la Peña: On the way from Py to Vernet-les-Bains
Tour to the highlight: https://www.komoot.de/tour/340851629?ref=itd
